title: Level 92
wikitext:
{{Class 1|entity=Non-Entity Hazards|hexagon=E|color-h=300|class=1e - Environmental|color-s=10%}} '''Level 92''' is the '''93rd''' level of [[The Backrooms]].{{Background|file=Level92 2.jpg}}
{{Gradient Header}} [[File:Level_92_1.png|center|700x700px|thumb|link=]]
[[File:Level_92_1.png|center|700x700px|thumb|link=]]
{{Audio|file=Phenom 15a thing.mp3|mono=yes|volume=1|toggle=8}} {{Collapse|num=6969|initial=
Play Audio?
|final=
|body=}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Level-92-3.jpg|caption=Photo of the forts of the level.}} '''Level 92''' is an expansive, interconnected network of blanket forts with undefined boundaries. These structures resemble subterranean caves in their irregular, terrain-like connections and tunnel-like formations. The general atmosphere is warm and inviting, with heated surfaces that evoke the sensation of lying atop a bed. The tunnels are typically wide enough to accommodate two to three individuals at once. However, if subjected to excessive weight, they may collapse downward, leading to deeper sections of the forts or, more frequently, dead ends. The forts are heavily furnished with blankets and pillows, in addition to various decorations and pieces of furniture. Most furniture is wooden, primarily consisting of seating arrangements and dressers, though small screens, fully stocked mini-fridges, functioning space heaters, and bean bag chairs are also present. The screens operate like televisions, perpetually displaying Christmas-themed broadcasts from television channels dating back to the level’s discovery in 2006. The most prominent decorative elements within the forts are their lighting arrangements. These typically consist of small fairy lights strung along the walls and edges, casting a warm yellow glow, as well as perpetually burning scented candles. Although instances of these candles igniting sections of the forts have been observed, such fires remain highly localized and tend to extinguish almost immediately. Additional decorations include plush animals, hand-knitted garments, remote controls, brooms, and similar miscellaneous objects. It is uncertain whether an external world exists beyond the forts. Cutting or removing a section of the structures generally leads to further forts or empty blanket fort corridors that end in dead ends. However, certain larger forts contain unbreakable windows that provide an external view. These windows are often accompanied by wooden furniture, such as chairs or dressers, and may feature candlelight or other forms of illumination. The scenes visible through them are either an empty void or, on rare occasions, a snow-covered forest landscape. This landscape is usually devoid of any significant features, though in the distance, a mountainous region can be seen, believed to correspond with [[Level 490]], along with structures associated with that level.
[[File:Level_92.png|center|1050x1050px|thumb|link=]]
[[File:Level_92.png|center|700x700px|thumb|link=]]
For most wanderers who enter '''Level 92''', it serves as a point of surrender. The level’s potent atmospheric allure has led to numerous expeditions being involuntarily abandoned, as wanderers become ensnared by its overwhelming sense of comfort. This effect causes individuals to wander aimlessly under the influence of the [[Phenomenon 15|Alone]] phenomenon until they reach a state of relaxation. Once a wanderer is experienced in a level's mentally debilitating effect, its progressive development more and more leads it into a more extensive network of forts—each new area more broad in scope than those that have welcomed wanderers who have experienced the same phenomenon. The growth eventually leads into an area that is not generally accessible, with a great supply shop as well as the single known location in which wanderers can socialize with each other. But leading towards this sector is followed by a growing sense of lethargy as well as mental fuzziness, induced by the phenomenon. While its full scope is not known, written notes left behind by affected individuals provide some insight into its nature. ==Additional Information== The following note was left by an unknown wanderer and is suggested to be connected to the level’s phenomenon.
File - Note.pdf "Never give up!" "Always face adversity head-on!" These words echoed in my mind dozens, hundreds—perhaps even thousands of times. Even as a child, at an age when I should have been playing, I was being molded for these ideals. And even though I now understand how deeply their eager words have worn me down, I still miss them. I never gave up. No matter how much I suffered, no matter how broken I became, a final sliver of hope within me always survived—always endured. But now, I realize how much it has begun to tear me apart. From the moment I arrived here, from the instant I stepped out of that damned office only to plummet into hell itself, then somehow return to that same accursed workplace, I thought my goal was always to keep moving forward. I was wrong. A store’s backrooms, parking garages, underground pipe tunnels, a station... Then beyond that, a bourgeois hotel, a pitch-black realm of secrets, and an endless ocean of thought. And in searching for answers within these thoughts, I reached caverns—a reflection of my own mind. But in the end, what I found was nothing like the answer I had expected. Enough cryptic speech—I should be clearer. Now I see that these caves were meant to be an ending. A realization that there would never be a final, absolute truth. No definitive right or wrong. That some questions were never meant to be answered. What I found was a realm of secrets. I returned to my childhood. I felt the weight of my burdens wash away, as if I had regained my purity. But no. This was not my childhood. This was the childhood I should have had. The life I should have lived. But I did what was right—or at least what I thought was right. Surviving. Making rational choices. As my caution slowly eroded, I felt warmth, serenity, nostalgia. And when I looked toward the exit, I saw the cruelty of reality. That was when I felt a fear unlike anything I had ever experienced before—the fear of losing this joy, of having these emotions stripped away. The fear of being reduced once again to a hollow puppet, burdened with responsibilities so heavy they could very well lead to my death. That old version of me is gone. That past self died in [[Level 8]], searching for the answers to life while drowning in meaningless duties and obligations. But I… I will stay here, forever, with my new family and friends. This is not the ending I expected—but it is the ending I deserve.
''- September 6, 2006.''
==Colonies and Outposts== The only known settlements within the level are small encampments established by lone wanderers. Larger factions, such as the [[M.E.G.]], have deliberately refused to set up an outpost within '''Level 92''', and as a result, no formal trade colonies or official bases exist. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== There is no definitive method of entry into '''Level 92'''. Instead, access appears to be contingent on specific conditions, often occurring when a wanderer experiences a profound sense of helplessness or disorientation within other levels. The earliest recorded instance of this phenomenon occurred in [[Level 8]], though entry is highly situational and lacks a precise triggering mechanism. ===Exits=== *Returning the way one came. *Performing a [[No-Clipping]] action through the exterior-facing windows has an equal probability of transporting the wanderer to [[Level 490]], or [[The Void]]. *[[Silent Horizon|''Hourglass will lead to the Canaan'']]. {{Redirect||Prev=91|Next=93}}
